definition: A three-to-four-way cross-asset pricing inconsistency where equities (21-22x P/E) price no recession and negative correlation, bonds (80-130bp term premium) price positive correlation, credit (IG OAS ~90-110bp) prices neither stress nor recession, and private credit (NAVs at par) prices neither defaults nor widening. Credit is the most mispriced leg.
evidence: Credit-equity basis (Merton-implied IG ~140-180bp vs. actual ~90-110bp) is widest since 2007
5/7 historical episodes resolved via credit widening
Credit inconsistent with BOTH regime outcomes: Regime D → spreads widen 200-400bp; Regime S → ROIC-WACC compression should produce wider spreads
open_questions: Is this mispricing or segmented equilibrium reflecting rational pricing by segmented investor bases?
What macro shock would narrow or widen the inconsistency?
Does 3+ years of persistence constitute evidence of equilibrium?
